commit.message:
Exclude NIST CTC fixtures from G4-vs-OCCT aggregate benchmark rows (#303)

* Initial plan

* Exclude NIST CTC (G4OCCTSolid) fixtures from G4-vs-OCCT aggregate benchmark rows

NIST CTC fixtures have no Geant4 native solid — both the "native" and
"imported" solids are the same G4OCCTSolid loaded from the same STEP
file.  Including them in the aggregate made the Geant4 baseline time
include slow OCCT evaluations, pushing the aggregate speedup ratio
toward 1 and masking the true cost of generality.

bench_navigator.cc: skip geant4_class=="G4OCCTSolid" fixtures for ray,
inside, and SN aggregate rows; print excluded-fixture count in header.

generate_bench_report.py: same exclusion logic in the Python report;
expose excluded_count in the data dict; render an explanatory note in
the aggregate section when fixtures are excluded.

commit.message:
perf: use per-face Hausdorff deflection in BVHLowerBoundDistance (#278)

* perf: use per-face Hausdorff deflection in BVHLowerBoundDistance

Previously, BVHLowerBoundDistance subtracted fBVHDeflection (=
kRelativeDeflection × maxFaceDiag, the global maximum over all faces)
from the BVH mesh distance to guarantee a conservative lower bound.
For multi-face solids where the largest face dominates fBVHDeflection
but the nearest triangle is on a smaller face, this over-subtracted the
correction and produced a looser lower bound than necessary, causing more
points to fall through to the expensive exact DistanceToOut solver.

Introduce fFaceDeflections: a per-face table mapping each face in
fFaceBoundsCache to kRelativeDeflection × face_bbox_diagonal.  After
the BVH traversal, use BRepExtrema_TriangleSet::GetFaceID(bestIdx) to
map the BVH-reordered triangle index back to its originating face, then
look up fFaceDeflections[faceId] for the tightest valid correction
instead of the global maximum.

This avoids the naive approach of pre-indexing by triangle: the BVH
build reorders triangles via Swap() (tracked in myTrgIdxMap), so
BestIndex() returns a post-reorder index that does not match a simple
per-triangle table built in face-traversal order.  GetFaceID() resolves
the reordering correctly.

Falls back to fBVHDeflection if BestIndex is unavailable or out of range.
fBVHDeflection is retained for the SurfaceNormal seed distance calculation.

DTO lower-bound violations remain 0 (verified by test suite).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: combine sanitizer and tsan jobs into a matrix job (#289)

Replace the separate `sanitizer` and `tsan` jobs with a single
`sanitizer` matrix job with two entries:
- `asan`: builds with -DUSE_ASAN=ON -DUSE_UBSAN=ON
- `tsan`: builds with -DUSE_TSAN=ON

All four sanitizer env vars (ASAN_OPTIONS, LSAN_OPTIONS, UBSAN_OPTIONS,
TSAN_OPTIONS) are defined unconditionally at job level; inactive
sanitizers simply ignore the unrelated variables.

The B1 multi-threaded run now executes in both matrix entries, providing
thread-safety coverage under both ASAN and TSAN.

The FetchContent cache key now includes the matrix sanitizer name to
avoid cross-entry cache collisions.

Also update AGENTS.md to reflect the new matrix structure and clarify
that tsan.supp is now a permanent suppression file (not conditional).

commit.message:
perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector in Inside() with BVH triangle ray-cast (#277)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector in Inside() with BVH triangle ray-cast

Replace the per-face IntCurvesFace_Intersector calls for curved faces in
G4OCCTSolid::Inside() with a single BVH traversal of the pre-built fTriangleSet
using Moller-Trumbore ray-triangle intersection.

Previously, every curved face invoked IntCurvesFace_Intersector::Perform()
individually. For a TwistedTubs solid with many curved faces, this resulted in
N_curved_faces expensive OCCT solver calls per Inside() query.

The new TriangleRayCast BVH traversal class casts the same +Z ray through the
BVH-accelerated triangle mesh (fTriangleSet) in a single O(log N_triangles)
traversal, counting crossings via Moller-Trumbore. Degenerate hits (ray near
triangle edge/vertex) fall back to BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier as before.
When fTriangleSet is unavailable, the original per-face path is retained as a
fallback.

Observed speedup in test suite wall-clock time: ~430 s -> ~62 s (7x) for the
full geometry validation run including TwistedTubs and ellipsoid fixtures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden TriangleRayCast and Inside() Tier-2a BVH path per review (#284)

* Initial plan

* fix: apply review feedback to TriangleRayCast and Inside() BVH path

  starts from a clean state (prevents misuse on reuse)

commit.message:
ci: extract RelWithDebInfo build into build-reldebinfo prerequisite job (#290)

* ci: extract RelWithDebInfo build into build-reldebinfo prerequisite job

The three callgrind profile jobs each ran a full CMake configure+build
before profiling. The matrix job rebuilt the same bench_callgrind binary
seven times (one per fixture), even though the fixture is only a runtime
argument.

Add a new build-reldebinfo job that:
- Configures with RelWithDebInfo, BUILD_BENCHMARKS=ON, BUILD_EXAMPLES=ON,
  ENABLE_WERROR=ON, B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF, and VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR.
- Builds all targets in one pass.
- Uploads bench_callgrind, bench_assembly_navigator, and exampleB4c as
  the reldebinfo-binaries artifact (retention 1 day).

All three callgrind jobs now declare needs: [build-reldebinfo], download
the artifact, and run only the profile + annotate steps inside eic_xl.

Also add -DB4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF to build-test-benchmark. This flag
only affects exampleB4c behavior and is safe to set globally.

commit.message:
bench: add NIST CTC timing benchmarks and point cloud generation (#292)

Replace the unconditional `continue` for IsImportedSelfComparisonFixture
(NIST CTC) with reduced-ray-count BM_rays and BM_inside benchmarks.
Using kNistCtcRayCount=64 rays avoids timeouts on large AP203 compound
assemblies while producing timing data and point cloud output.

Since native and imported are the same G4OCCTSolid for CTC fixtures,
mismatches are always 0.  The benchmarks wrap CompareFixtureRays in a
try-catch so a G4Exception on a pathological CTC geometry does not abort
the entire benchmark run.

fix: room color formula uses temp+humidity as primary drivers

Light was identical across floors (same HA sensor entity used).
New formula: temperature and humidity are primary color drivers,
light only adds overall brightness. This guarantees visible color
differences even when lux readings are the same.

- Bright adds lightness to all channels equally

fix: much more distinctive room colors

Old formula: fill(12+tTemp*18, 14+tLight*14, 18+coolBias*12)
-> all values 12-30, rooms looked identical dark grey

- Eerste verd. (18C, 422lux, 50%): fill(110, 101, 135) cooler blue-purple

Root causes:
1. Initial seeding: 12 predators = 12 herbivores per room (1:1 ratio kills herbivores fast)
2. Predator lifespan: 800-1400 frames = 13-23s (dead before they can eat)

- Initial counts per room: plants=18, herbivores=8, predators=3 (was 12), decomp=6, fungus=6
- Predator lifespan: 800-1400 -> 3000-6000 frames
- Predator energy drain: -0.04/frame -> -0.015/frame
- Predator hunt radius: 110 -> 80px, steering: 0.35 -> 0.20
- Herbivore flee: stronger force (0.08 close, 0.01 far), wider range (80->100px)
- Herbivore reproduction: threshold 115->95 energy, chance 0.003->0.005
- Added checkMinPopulation(): runs every 5s, auto-spawns if species < critical minimum

Fix door migration: cells now actually pass through doors

and the 0.3% random migration chance was far too low.

  inside a doorway opening (isInDoorZoneX() helper)
- checkDoorMigration() detects when cell has entered the gap zone
  (the physical space between floors) and teleports it deterministically
  — no random chance needed, cells just drift through naturally

commit.message:
Add college-level SYS 202 lesson YAML files for Linux admin topics

Write three detailed YAML lesson files for SYS 202 (Linux System Administration):

- 01_essential_commands.yaml (548 lines): Bash CLI rationale, command anatomy,
  filesystem navigation (interactive terminal widget), file operations, cat/less/
  head/tail/wc, grep with all flags, permissions with octal/symbolic chmod
  (interactive chmod_calc widget), and process management. Includes Mermaid
  filesystem-navigation diagram and 9-bit permission diagram. 4 quiz questions.

- 02_filesystem.yaml (418 lines): Unix filesystem history, FHS 3.0 directory
  reference with full Mermaid graph TD tree (2+ levels), inode internals with
  hard-link vs. symlink Mermaid diagram, /etc/fstab format with annotated
  examples, /proc and /sys virtual filesystems, tmpfs, and disk management
  commands (lsblk, fdisk, blkid, df, du). 4 quiz questions.

- 02_networking_commands.yaml (573 lines): iproute2 vs. net-tools mapping table,
  ip addr/route/link output decoded, ping/traceroute with Mermaid sequence
  diagram (interactive terminal widget), DNS tools (dig sections explained,
  resolv.conf, /etc/hosts), ss -tulnp with TCP state reference and ss vs.
  netstat comparison table, tcpdump and nc, SSH key setup and config file,
  OSI-layer troubleshooting Mermaid flowchart. 4 quiz questions.

All files pass yaml.safe_load() validation and all 48 existing tests pass.
References: Shotts 'The Linux Command Line', FHS 3.0 (Linux Foundation),
POSIX IEEE Std 1003.1, RFC 792/4251/1034, Love 'Linux Kernel Development'.

commit.message:
Modernize type hints across ignite codebase to Python 3.10+ style (#3689)

The codebase still used legacy `typing` module annotations (`Optional`,
`Union`, `List`, `Dict`, `Tuple`, `Callable`, `Sequence`, etc.) that
have been superseded by built-in generics and `|` union syntax since
Python 3.10. Since ignite requires `>=3.10`, these can all be replaced.

## Changes

- **`Optional[X]` → `X | None`, `Union[X, Y]` → `X | Y`** across
affected files (via `pyupgrade --py310-plus`)
- **`Callable`, `Sequence`, `Iterable`, `Mapping` moved from `typing` →
`collections.abc`** — their canonical home; `typing` versions are
aliases kept only for legacy compat
- **`super(ClassName, self).method()` → `super().method()`** — redundant
args in single-inheritance Python 3 code
- **Removed `pass` from abstract methods that have docstrings** —
docstring alone is a valid body
- **Removed `# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-`** — UTF-8 is the default in Python
3

### Before / After example

```python
# Before
from typing import Callable, Optional, Sequence

def attach(self, engine: "Engine", output_transform: Callable = lambda x: x,
           device: Optional[str] = None, names: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None) -> None:

# After
from collections.abc import Callable, Sequence

def attach(self, engine: "Engine", output_transform: Callable = lambda x: x,
           device: str | None = None, names: Sequence[str] | None = None) -> None:
```

28 files updated in total, all within `ignite/`.

The RegistryMap inner type was HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> which
has non-deterministic iteration order (Rust's RandomState). When
minecraft:dimension_type entries were sent in random order during
configuration, the hardcoded dimension_type_id=0 in the login packet
would randomly map to the wrong dimension type (e.g. the_nether with
min_y=0 and no skylight instead of overworld with min_y=-64).

This caused intermittent startup issues where:
- Chunks appeared to start from Y=0 instead of Y=-64
- Skybox rendering was wrong
- Blocks were not breakable

The fix:
1. Replace inner HashMap with IndexMap to preserve JSON insertion order
   (matching vanilla's registration order)
2. Add get_registry_entry_index() to look up entry indices by name
3. Replace hardcoded dimension_type_id=0 with dynamic lookup

BREAKING CHANGE: indexmap workspace dep now requires serde feature

refactor(server): config cleanup — remove Java leftovers & extract hardcoded values

Part A: Remove 3 Java-specific config fields with no Rust equivalent:
- is_jmx_monitoring_enabled (Java JMX monitoring API)
- is_native_transport_enabled (Netty epoll/kqueue toggle)
- is_sync_chunk_writes (Java threading model toggle)

Part B: Extract ~25 hardcoded constants into config structs:
- NetworkTimeoutsConfig: keepalive interval/timeout, login/configuration/write timeouts
- RateLimitConfig: max connections per window, window duration, cleanup interval
- Channel capacities: inbound (128) and outbound (512) channel buffer sizes
- EntityTrackingConfig: per-type tracking ranges (player/animal/monster/misc/projectile/default)
- WeatherConfig: rain and thunder delay/duration min/max values
- World tuning: chunk_cache_size (1024) and max_concurrent_chunk_generations (64)

All constants preserved as documented defaults. Validation ensures keepalive_timeout
>= keepalive_interval, weather min <= max, and positive cache/concurrency values.

Closes R5.18.

feat(world,protocol): complete R5.16 safety hardening & cleanup

- Bound all network-facing NBT reads with default_quota() (2 MiB)
  instead of unlimited(), matching vanilla's network NBT limit
  (read_component_nbt + clientbound_registry_data decode)

- Update fluid/ticking counters in chunk sections:
  - set_block_state() now maintains fluid_count, ticking_block_count,
    and ticking_fluid_count via BlockStateId property lookups
  - recalculate_counts() recalculates all four counters
  - from_parts() calls recalculate_counts() for full accuracy
  - filled() computes correct counters for the fill block
  - Add PalettedContainer::for_each_value() for efficient iteration
  - 14 new unit tests for counter transitions

- Evaluate reqwest: decision to keep (documented in auth.rs module doc)
  Only 1 HTTP call, all features required, switching breaks public API

- Items 2/3/4 (rate limiter disconnect, player removal, mining_pos)
  were already implemented in prior phases
